questions.stem: This Gram stain shows round purple organisms arranged in irregular grape-like clusters. Which genus does this arrangement suggest?

explanation.summary: Gram-positive cocci that bunch into grape-like clusters are characteristic of Staphylococcus, which divides in multiple planes. Streptococci form chains, Neisseria form diplococci, and Bacillus/Clostridium are rods.
explanation.whyCorrect: Gram-positive cocci that bunch into grape-like clusters are characteristic of Staphylococcus, which divides in multiple planes. Streptococci form chains, Neisseria form diplococci, and Bacillus/Clostridium are rods.

questions.stem: These white, dry, wrinkled colonies grew on Sabouraud dextrose agar. What type of organism is this medium designed to select for?

explanation.summary: Sabouraud dextrose agar uses an acidic pH plus antibiotics to inhibit bacteria and favor the growth of fungi such as Candida and Aspergillus.
explanation.whyCorrect: Sabouraud dextrose agar uses an acidic pH plus antibiotics to inhibit bacteria and favor the growth of fungi such as Candida and Aspergillus.

questions.stem: A rectal swab was plated on the two media shown; growth is abundant on the left plate but restricted to a specific organism on the right. What property of the right-hand medium explains this?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is a nonselective enrichment broth

choices.label: E
choices.text: It supports all normal flora equally

explanation.summary: Thayer-Martin medium contains antibiotics that suppress normal flora and select for Neisseria gonorrhoeae, whereas the nonselective chocolate agar is overgrown by normal flora.
explanation.whyCorrect: Thayer-Martin medium contains antibiotics that suppress normal flora and select for Neisseria gonorrhoeae, whereas the nonselective chocolate agar is overgrown by normal flora.

questions.stem: This organism produced a distinctive bright metallic-green sheen on EMB agar. Which bacterium is classically identified by this appearance?

explanation.summary: Rapid, vigorous lactose fermentation by E. coli produces amines that bind the dyes, creating the characteristic green metallic sheen on EMB.
explanation.whyCorrect: Rapid, vigorous lactose fermentation by E. coli produces amines that bind the dyes, creating the characteristic green metallic sheen on EMB.

questions.stem: This gram-positive algorithm sorts beta-hemolytic streptococci by antibiotic susceptibility. Sensitivity to which agent identifies Streptococcus pyogenes?

explanation.summary: S. pyogenes (Group A) is bacitracin sensitive, whereas S. agalactiae (Group B) is bacitracin resistant. Optochin and novobiocin are used for other organisms.
explanation.whyCorrect: S. pyogenes (Group A) is bacitracin sensitive, whereas S. agalactiae (Group B) is bacitracin resistant. Optochin and novobiocin are used for other organisms.

questions.stem: In this test, one sample bubbles vigorously when mixed with hydrogen peroxide while the other does not. Which gram-positive genus corresponds to the bubbling (positive) result?

explanation.summary: Catalase-positive staphylococci break H2O2 into water and oxygen, producing bubbles; streptococci are catalase negative and produce none.
explanation.whyCorrect: Catalase-positive staphylococci break H2O2 into water and oxygen, producing bubbles; streptococci are catalase negative and produce none.

questions.stem: A disk was placed on this blood agar plate; one half shows a clear zone of inhibition around the disk and the other grows up to it. If the disk contains optochin, which organism is represented by the zone of inhibition?

explanation.summary: S. pneumoniae is optochin sensitive, producing a clear zone of inhibition, whereas viridans streptococci are optochin resistant and grow up to the disk.
explanation.whyCorrect: S. pneumoniae is optochin sensitive, producing a clear zone of inhibition, whereas viridans streptococci are optochin resistant and grow up to the disk.

questions.stem: This gram-negative identification decision tree branches early on a single enzyme test. Which test distinguishes Pseudomonas from most Enterobacteriaceae here?

explanation.summary: Pseudomonas is oxidase positive while most Enterobacteriaceae (E. coli, Shigella, Salmonella) are oxidase negative, making oxidase the key early branch.
explanation.whyCorrect: Pseudomonas is oxidase positive while most Enterobacteriaceae (E. coli, Shigella, Salmonella) are oxidase negative, making oxidase the key early branch.

questions.stem: In this assay, one filter-paper sample developed a dark blue-purple color while the other stayed pale. The blue color indicates the organism produces which enzyme?

explanation.summary: The oxidase reagent turns blue-purple when oxidized by cytochrome c oxidase, marking an oxidase-positive organism; a pale result is oxidase negative.
explanation.whyCorrect: The oxidase reagent turns blue-purple when oxidized by cytochrome c oxidase, marking an oxidase-positive organism; a pale result is oxidase negative.
